reasoning: The market price was 0.85 on May 29. Late-May news reveals highly active and late-stage negotiations: Israel reportedly accepted a temporary ceasefire proposal on May 30, and Hamas responded with requested amendments on May 31. While this indicates an immediate deal in early June was slightly delayed by haggling over details, the fact that proposals are actively being exchanged and accepted by Israel points to a high likelihood of an eventual agreement. Given that this market resolves YES if *any* official ceasefire is agreed to before December 31, 2025 (meaning they still have almost seven full months) and they already successfully agreed to a temporary ceasefire earlier this year (Jan 15), the probability remains very strongly in favor. I am slightly discounting the 85% market peak to 82% to account for the risk of talks breaking down due to Hamas's May 31 amendment demands.
